name: Elk Falls
description: One of the most accessible and photographed falls in the High Country. A short flat trail from the Elk River Road parking area leads to a dramatic 50-foot plunge into a deep swimming hole.

name: Linville Falls
description: The most visited waterfall along the Blue Ridge Parkway. The Linville River plunges through a rugged gorge over two distinct drops into Linville Gorge, managed by the National Park Service. Multiple overlook trails available.

name: Crabtree Falls
description: A 70-foot curtain falls tucked into a narrow gorge off the Blue Ridge Parkway at Milepost 339.5. The loop trail descends steeply through a hardwood cove; seasonal wildflowers are exceptional in spring.

name: Cascades Falls
description: A dramatic 200-foot multi-tiered cascade in Doughton Park on the Blue Ridge Parkway (Milepost 271.9). The Falls Creek Trail offers a gentle descent through an open meadow before entering forest.

name: Upper Creek Falls
description: A series of cascades and a primary 60-foot drop on Upper Creek in the Pisgah National Forest. The trail descends sharply into a rugged gorge with several creek crossings — challenging after rain.

name: Grandfather Falls
description: A 35-foot horsetail falls on Boone Fork near the base of Grandfather Mountain State Park. Accessed via the Nuwati Trail; the falls are framed by massive boulders and rhododendron thickets.

name: Lost Cove Cliffs Falls
description: A roadside cascade just off NC 181 near the Pisgah National Forest boundary. The falls are visible from a small pullout; a short scramble leads to the base. Best after rain.

name: Laurel Fork Falls
description: A 40-foot plunge on Laurel Fork Creek in the Stone Mountain State Park area of Ashe County. The creek runs year-round and the trail passes through a mix of hemlock groves and open hardwood forest.
